data.title: Star Group, L.P. to Host Fiscal 2025 Third Quarter Webcast and Conference Call August 7, 2025
data.description:
STAMFORD, Conn., July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Star Group, L.P. (the “Company” or “Star”) (NYSE: SGU), a leading home energy distributor and services provider, today announced that it will release its fiscal 2025 third quarter results after the close of trading on August 6, 2025. Members of Star's management team will host a webcast and conference call at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time the following day, August 7, 2025, to review the three and nine months ended June 30, 2025.

The webcast will be accessible on the company’s website, at www.stargrouplp.com, and the telephone number for the conference call is 888-346-3470 (412-317-5169 for international callers).

About Star Group, L.P.
Star Group, L.P. is a full service provider specializing in the sale of home heating products and services to residential and commercial customers to heat their homes and buildings. The Company also sells and services heating and air conditioning equipment to its home heating oil and propane customers and, to a lesser extent, provides these offerings to customers outside of its home heating oil and propane customer base. Star also sells diesel, gasoline and home heating oil on a delivery only basis. We believe Star is the nation's largest retail distributor of home heating oil based upon sales volume. Including its propane locations, Star serves customers in the more northern and eastern states within the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic U.S. regions. Additional information is available by obtaining the Company's SEC filings at www.sec.gov and by visiting Star's website at www.stargrouplp.com, where unit holders may request a hard copy of Star’s complete audited financial statements free of charge.

Forward-Looking Information
This news release includes "forward-looking statements" which represent the Company’s expectations or beliefs concerning future events that involve risks and uncertainties, including the impact of geopolitical events on wholesale product cost volatility, tariff regimes, including newly imposed U.S. tariffs and any additional responsive non-U.S. tariffs or additional U.S. tariffs, the price and supply of the products that we sell, the price and supply of the products that we sell, our ability to purchase sufficient quantities of product to meet our customer’s needs, rapid increases in levels of inflation, the consumption patterns of our customers, our ability to obtain satisfactory gross profit margins, the effect of weather conditions on our financial performance, our ability to obtain new customers and retain existing customers, our ability to make strategic acquisitions, the impact of litigation, natural gas conversions and electrification of heating systems, pandemic and future global health pandemics, recessionary economic conditions, future union relations and the outcome of current and future union negotiations, the impact of current and future governmental regulations, including climate change, environmental, health, and safety regulations, the ability to attract and retain employees, customer credit worthiness, counterparty credit worthiness, marketing plans, cyber-attacks, global supply chain issues, labor shortages and new technology, including alternative methods for heating and cooling residences. All statements other than statements of historical facts included in this Report including, without limitation, the statements under “Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations” and elsewhere herein, are forward-looking statements. Without limiting the foregoing, the words “believe,” “anticipate,” “plan,” “expect,” “seek,” “estimate,” and similar expressions are intended to identify forward-looking statements. Although we believe that the expectations reflected in such forward-looking statements are reasonable, we can give no assurance that such expectations will prove to be correct. Actual results may differ materially from those projected as a result of certain risks and uncertainties. These risks and uncertainties include, but are not limited to, those set forth under the heading "Risk Factors" and "Business Strategy" in our Annual Report on Form 10-K (the "Form 10-K") for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2024. Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from the Company’s expectations ("Cautionary Statements") are disclosed in this news release and in the Company’s Form 10-K and our Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q. All subsequent written and oral forward-looking statements attributable to the Company or persons acting on its behalf are expressly qualified in their entirety by the Cautionary Statements. Unless otherwise required by law, the Company undertakes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise after the date of this news release.

CONTACT: 
Star GroupChris Witty
Investor RelationsDarrow Associates, Inc.
203/328-7310646/438-9385 or cwitty@darrowir.com

data.title: Trimology Weight Loss Reviews (EXPOSED) Can This BioSignal Supplement Really Flatten Your Belly Without Starving?
data.description:

>> Your Gut, Hormones, And Metabolism Have Been Ignored For Too Long, See If Trimology Changes That.

One woman wrote, "I don't know how else to describe it… it's like my body finally remembered how to burn fat. I was doing everything right before, and nothing worked. With the og trimology, I just feel normal again."

That "normal" feeling is what sets Trimology apart. It's not designed to force weight loss like harsh stimulants or keto hacks. Instead, it rebuilds microbial balance and hormonal signaling, what the company calls "restoring the BioSignal Network", so your metabolism can naturally regulate hunger, inflammation, and energy usage again.

And With Over 147,000 Women Reportedly Using The Supplement By Mid-2025, the demand, and the real-life results, are impossible to ignore.

>>Ready To Try Trimology For Yourself? It's Currently 73% Off On The Official Website With A 180-Day Refund Guarantee.

Unlike most weight loss supplements that take months to show anything, or worse, deliver nothing at all, Trimology results tend to show up in layers. Verified users report changes not just on the scale, but in how they feel day to day. From energy levels and bloating to mood and sugar cravings, the benefits compound over time. Here's what real Trimology before and after experiences look like across key checkpoints.

After 7 Days: Most women describe a rapid change in digestion. The common terms are "lighter," "less puffy," and "finally regular." Many say afternoon crashes disappeared, and cravings, especially for sugar, dropped significantly. One verified review on day 6 said, "I didn't realize how bloated I was until I wasn't."

>> You Don't Have To Decide Now-Just Try It, Watch What Happens, And Claim Your Refund If Needed.

After 30 Days: This is when fat loss starts to show, not just in pounds but inches. Waist measurements, stubborn belly fat, and hormonal bloating often reduce. Some users report 4-7 pounds lost with no major diet changes. But more importantly, energy is back, focus improves, and women say they feel like themselves again. A woman in perimenopause wrote, "I couldn't stick to anything before. Trimology made it effortless."

After 60 Days: Metabolism appears to fully "reboot" in many women. This is when the fat-burning momentum kicks in, and even previously resistant areas like hips and thighs begin to shrink. Many report "eating normally" yet still leaning out, something they thought was impossible over age 35.

By 90 Days: The results solidify. Energy is steady, hunger is in check, and users say it feels like a new normal. One woman wrote: "I'm not dieting. I'm just not struggling anymore." That's the power of the BioSignal reset, your body remembers how to function without being forced.

>>If You're Even Curious Whether Trimology Could Reset Your Metabolism Too, It's Now 73% Off With A Full 180-Day Money-Back Guarantee From The Official Website.

When you search Trimology reviews 2025, this consistent timeline comes up again and again. Real women. Real metabolism recovery. No starving. No side effects.

Trimology Benefits for Women Over 35: Metabolism, Cravings, Hormones & Belly Fat

What makes Trimology different isn't just the ingredients, it's the specific benefits designed for women over 35. While most fat burners ignore the hormonal and microbial shifts that happen with age, Trimology confronts them head-on by targeting the root cause: disrupted BioSignal communication.

Here are the most consistently reported Trimology benefits based on user reviews, testing feedback, and biological mechanisms:

1. Restored Metabolic Function
Trimology helps the body remember how to burn fat by restoring short-chain fatty acid production, especially butyrate. This compound is essential for regulating blood sugar, inflammation, and mitochondrial energy output. As users regain microbial diversity, metabolism naturally kicks back into gear.

With more butyrate and improved gut-brain signaling, women report significantly fewer cravings, especially for sugar, carbs, and late-night snacks. This helps cut excess calories without needing iron-willed willpower.

>> Try Trimology On Your Schedule For 73% Off. If It's Not Different, Don't Keep It (180-Day Money Back Guarantee).

The probiotic-resistance starch combo improves digestion within days. Bloating, irregularity, and water retention ease quickly, leading to a visibly flatter stomach even before measurable fat loss.

Trimology may support the hormonal transitions that occur in perimenopause and menopause. Users report fewer mood swings, steadier energy, and easier fat loss, especially around the belly, where cortisol-induced fat tends to accumulate.

>>Women Over 35 Are Seeing Real Metabolic Change With Trimology, And With 73% Off Plus A Full 180-Day Guarantee, There's Nothing To Lose By Trying It.

The question on many women's minds before trying any supplement is simple: Is Trimology safe? That alone eliminates the top 4 causes of common weight loss pill side effects like racing heart, insomnia, dehydration, and digestive upset. Instead, Trimology relies entirely on naturally occurring prebiotics, probiotics, and microbiome-supportive compounds to restore metabolic balance.

The two main active strains, Clostridium butyricum and Akkermansia muciniphila, have both been studied extensively for their role in gut health and inflammation regulation. In medical literature, they're considered well-tolerated and beneficial to overall metabolic and immune function. Clinical research has shown that butyrate-producing bacteria can improve glucose control and lower systemic inflammation, without causing digestive issues.

>> Try Trimology Today-Real Fat Loss Starts At The Signal, And It's 100% Guaranteed.

The resistant starch blend sourced from potato extract and chicory root is also widely recognized as a functional, gut-friendly fiber. It feeds healthy microbes, improves bowel regularity, and enhances nutrient absorption, all without triggering bloating when dosed correctly, as it is here.

Still, you should always consult your physician if you're:

That said, Trimology has been used safely by women from perimenopause to post-menopause, with no serious side effects reported. In fact, most users report the exact opposite: less bloating, more energy, better digestion, improved mental clarity, and effortless appetite regulation.

>>Trimology Is Made In A Gmp-Certified, Fda-Inspected Facility, And Every Order Comes With A Full 180-Day Money-Back Guarantee, So You Have Nothing To Lose By Trying It Today (Click Here).

How Trimology Works: The BioSignal Network, Resistant Starch & Butyrate Explained
Forget what you think you know about fat burners. Trimology doesn't rely on caffeine, stimulants, or appetite suppressants. Instead, it targets the root mechanism behind slow metabolism in women over 35: a breakdown in the BioSignal Network.

What exactly is the BioSignal Network? It's the internal communication loop between your gut, brain, hormones, and fat cells. This network controls how your body stores fat, when it feels full, and how it responds to inflammation. But according to research cited on Trimology's official site, this signaling network weakens as women age, especially after 35, because key butyrate-producing microbes start disappearing from the gut.

Trimology works by restoring this microbial diversity and fueling it with resistant starch, specifically from non-GMO potato extract and chicory root. These prebiotic compounds feed "good" bacteria like Clostridium butyricum and Akkermansia muciniphila, which in turn produce butyrate, a short-chain fatty acid that helps regulate inflammation, control cravings, and ignite mitochondrial fat burning.

>> You Can Try It For 180 Days. If Nothing Changes, You Lose Nothing. But What If It Works?

When butyrate levels rise, the body starts to self-correct:

  • Cravings drop.

  • Blood sugar stabilizes.

  • Cortisol (stress fat hormone) reduces.

  • Energy becomes sustainable throughout the day.

The "Signal Reset Triad" in Trimology also supports gut lining integrity and female hormone sensitivity, two areas often ignored in traditional weight loss products. Rather than crash dieting, users simply allow their metabolism to "remember" how to function, no forcing, no starving.

>>Curious Whether Your Own Biosignal Network Is The Problem? Trimology Is Now Up To 73% Off With Free Shipping And A Full 180-Day Guarantee From The Official Site.

Trimology Price Breakdown, Website Discounts & 180-Day Refund Policy
If you're considering Trimology, you'll want to understand the pricing model, refund policy, and where to safely order it, because there are fake versions being sold through 3rd parties.

Trimology Ingredients Breakdown: What's Inside the BioSignal Reset Formula?
One of the biggest reasons women are trusting this supplement in 2025 is the transparency and science behind the Trimology ingredients. Unlike generic fat burners filled with caffeine or sketchy proprietary blends, Trimology leans on a unique microbial restoration complex designed to revive what science calls the "BioSignal Network." This internal system, connecting your gut, hormones, and metabolism, can't function without specific bacterial strains and their metabolic outputs, particularly butyrate.

At the heart of the Trimology formula is a rare but medically studied strain: Clostridium butyricum. Known as a butyrate-producing microbe, it feeds off prebiotic fibers and produces short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) that are essential for gut lining repair, hunger regulation, and fat metabolism. Studies show that low butyrate production is a common feature in women struggling with metabolic slowdown, especially post-35.

The second star of the formula is Akkermansia muciniphila, a probiotic strain shown to improve insulin sensitivity, reduce fat storage, and lower inflammation. It's not commonly found in most probiotics due to stability issues, but Trimology uses a stabilized form, making it bioavailable in capsule format.

>> Now's The Time To Reset Your Metabolism-73% Off And Nothing To Lose For 6 Months.

These probiotics are then paired with a resistant starch complex made from non-GMO potato extract and chicory root fiber. Trimology's proprietary Signal Reset Triad also includes additional hormonal and gut-supportive agents, although the exact blend remains trade-secreted. Women are no longer satisfied with vague weight loss pills. The Trimology ingredients offer real science, clinical mechanisms, and a formula built around restoring function, not forcing short-term results through artificial means.

>>You Can View The Full Trimology Formula On The Official Site, Bottles Are Up To 73% Off With A 180-Day Guarantee While Supplies Last.

Trimology Dosage Guide: How to Take It, Mistakes to Avoid & What to Expect First
The beauty of Trimology isn't just its science-backed ingredients, it's the simplicity of the routine. Unlike fat burners that require awkward meal timing or daily shakes, Trimology fits easily into your morning schedule.

According to the manufacturer and verified user feedback, here's the Trimology dosage and optimal usage breakdown:

What to Expect First:

This gentle but steady improvement Reflects The Trimology Biosignal Reset Process, not a crash diet or stimulant rush. By supporting microbial rebalance and hormonal signaling, the benefits accumulate rather than spike and crash.

Bottom Line? If You Want To Be Sure You're Getting The Authentic Trimology Biosignal Reset Formula, Always Go Through The Official Source.

Who Should Use Trimology? Ideal Users, Men vs Women, and Who It's NOT For
Trimology was originally designed with women over 35 in mind, especially those experiencing unexplained weight gain, slow metabolism, hormonal changes, and digestive irregularity. These symptoms often stem from disruptions in the gut-brain-hormone connection known as the BioSignal Network.

So, who exactly is Trimology for?

Ideal Candidates:

  • Women 35+ in perimenopause or menopause

  • Mothers post-pregnancy struggling to lose belly fat

  • Anyone with stubborn fat gain despite exercise and "clean" eating

  • Women with sugar cravings or emotional eating tendencies

  • Those dealing with bloating, constipation, or slow digestion

If you've tried calorie tracking, intermittent fasting, cardio, keto, or meal replacement shakes with no real success, or success that never lasted, then Trimology was likely designed for you.

It doesn't override your biology. It works with it. By supporting butyrate production, leptin signaling, and gut integrity, it unlocks the fat-burning and appetite-regulating signals your body has been missing.

>> Trimology Is The Only Formula That Gives Your Metabolism A Real Second Chance-With No Financial Risk.

What About Men?
Yes, men can absolutely use Trimology. While it's tailored to women's metabolic shifts, the mechanism, rebuilding the BioSignal Network, is gender-neutral. In fact, many female users report that their husbands "borrowed" their supply and noticed improvements in digestion, appetite control, and midsection fat.

Still, it's best used by those who:

  • Struggle with hormonal weight gain

  • Feel like their metabolism is "stuck in reverse"

Who Shouldn't Use It?

  • Children under 18

  • Women who are currently pregnant or nursing (pending doctor approval)

  • People on immunosuppressants (check with your physician)

Other than that, the safety record and broad benefits make Trimology a great fit for nearly anyone stuck in the frustrating cycle of yo-yo dieting, metabolic resistance, or sugar-driven fatigue.

>>Ready To See If Trimology Could Reset Your Metabolism Too? Bottles Are Up To 73% Off Today With A 180-Day Refund Guarantee, Only From The Official Website.

Trimology for Hormonal Belly Fat, Bloating & Sugar Cravings: Can It Really Help?
Trimology wasn't built to just burn calories. It was designed to restore the internal signal breakdown that causes hormonal belly fat, uncontrollable cravings, and frustrating bloating, especially in women over 35.

If you've ever thought:

  • "Why am I gaining weight even when I'm eating less?"

  • "Why does my belly look bloated no matter what I eat?"

  • "Why do I lose control around sugar, especially at night?"

Then the answer might not be willpower, it might be hormonal signaling collapse, and that's exactly what Trimology targets.

What Causes Hormonal Weight Gain?
As estrogen and progesterone fluctuate during perimenopause and menopause, they trigger:

  • Leptin resistance, which disrupts appetite signals

  • Insulin spikes, which increase belly fat storage

  • Cortisol dominance, which promotes inflammation and stubborn midsection weight

  • Cravings for fast carbs, driven by gut-brain miscommunication

>> Still Not Convinced? That's What The 180-Day Refund Is For-Either It Works, Or It Doesn't.

Trimology's Signal Reset Triad was specifically formulated to interrupt these feedback loops. By nourishing the gut with resistant starch (from potato and chicory root), it helps stabilize blood sugar, reduce cortisol levels, and calm the inflammation that drives bloat.

It also boosts butyrate production, which has been linked in clinical studies to:

  • Balanced mood and fewer energy crashes

Thousands of verified women are now calling Trimology their "craving killer" and "bloat fix" because of these shifts. What's remarkable is how many noticed the change before they lost weight:

"The first thing I noticed wasn't the scale, it was that I didn't need a nap, didn't need chocolate at 3 p.m., and my jeans didn't feel tight around my waist."

That's the Trimology difference, it doesn't force weight loss. It gently reminds your body how to regulate itself again.

>>If You've Been Stuck In A Hormonal Plateau, Trimology Is Now Up To 73% Off From The Official Site, Plus, Every Order Is Backed By A 180-Day No-Questions Refund Policy.

How Trimology Compares to Other Weight Loss Supplements in 2025
2025 has brought a flood of new fat loss supplements, but very few of them tackle the root cause of modern weight gain the way Trimology does. Most rely on outdated methods:

  • Stimulant-heavy thermogenesis

  • Temporary appetite suppression

  • Water loss masquerading as fat loss

  • Caffeine + green tea + CLA blends repackaged as "metabolic hacks"

Trimology is different. It's not about forcing your body to burn fat. It's about restoring the BioSignal Network, the communication system between your gut, brain, hormones, and fat cells. When that network is broken, no diet or workout will fix the problem long-term.

Here's what makes Trimology stand out from 90% of its competitors:

Feature

Trimology

Typical Weight Loss Pills

Gut microbiome support

(Resistant starch, Akkermansia, butyrate producers)

What you get is not a stimulant rush, but a metabolic reprogramming. One that's especially impactful for women whose biology has shifted due to age, stress, or hormonal fluctuations.

And unlike single-focus pills that spike your heart rate or dehydrate you, Trimology helps your body repair the signaling breakdown that caused the gain in the first place.

>>If You're Tired Of Taking Fat Burners That Leave You Wired But No Lighter, Trimology Might Be The Gut Reset You've Needed All Along. 73% Off Today Via The Official Website, With A 6-Month, Risk-Free Guarantee.

Trimology vs Ozempic, Keto & Calorie Tracking: Is This a Better Long-Term Approach?
It's no secret that many women over 35 have tried every diet under the sun, keto, fasting, tracking every macro, and some are even turning to injectable GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic just to get results. But here's the problem:

These approaches often ignore the real reason your body is gaining weight, your BioSignal Network is broken.

Let's compare Trimology's approach with three of the most popular weight loss strategies of the last decade:

Trimology vs Ozempic (Semaglutide)

  • Ozempic forces appetite suppression via synthetic GLP-1 mimicking

  • Comes with side effects like nausea, vomiting, gallbladder risk, and potential rebound weight gain once discontinued

  • Expensive ($900+/month) and requires ongoing injections

Trimology, on the other hand, boosts your own GLP-1 signaling naturally, via butyrate production from gut microbiota like Clostridium butyricum. It doesn't shut off appetite, it restores fullness cues and craving control from the inside.

Trimology vs Keto & Calorie Counting

  • Keto forces extreme restriction, often backfires with hormonal imbalance and fatigue

  • Calorie counting is mentally exhausting and doesn't solve metabolic dysfunction

  • Both methods can result in rebound gain once "normal" eating resumes

Trimology doesn't rely on restriction. It re-educates your metabolism by improving gut-brain-hormone communication. That's the signal shift that makes weight regulation feel natural again, not like a battle.

Here's how users summarize it:

"I lost 11 lbs on keto, but gained 13 back. With Trimology, I lost slower… but I never gained it back. I didn't feel deprived. And I didn't have to track a single calorie."

Ultimately, Trimology is the first supplement to challenge the "restrict and hope" model, offering a sustainable, science-backed path that works with your biology, not against it.

>>Don't Gamble With Injections Or Starvation Plans. Try Trimology Today With A 180-Day Refund Guarantee, And See What Happens When Your Metabolism Finally Starts Listening Again.

Does Trimology Help Prevent Weight Regain? The Science Behind Long-Term BioSignal Repair
The harsh truth about most weight loss solutions? They stop working the minute you stop using them. That's because they never fix the real problem, they only mask it.

Trimology was designed differently. It targets your BioSignal Network, which controls:

  • Hunger vs satiety

  • Fat storage vs fat burn

  • Gut inflammation vs microbiome harmony

  • Energy crashes vs metabolic flow

When that system gets repaired, from the inside out, it creates the conditions for long-term stability. That's why many Trimology users report not just initial weight loss… but no rebound months after stopping.

Why Is This Important?
Rebound weight gain isn't about willpower, it's about broken biological feedback loops. After extreme diets or semaglutide injections, those loops stay disrupted.

But Trimology's gut repair agents, resistant starch, butyrate boosters, and probiotic strains like Akkermansia, help rebuild the microbiota that regulate leptin, insulin, GLP-1, and cortisol. This, in turn, reduces:

  • Sugar cravings

  • Bloating

  • Mood-driven eating

data.title: CVS Health Shares Up on 2Q Results Beating Expectations, Raised Guidance
data.description:
By Denny Jacob 




  CVS Health shares rose 2.7% after quarterly results beat Wall Street's estimates and the company raised its outlook for the year. 



  Shares were trading around $63.97. The stock is up about 42% on the year. 



  The healthcare giant reported net income of $1.02 billion on revenue of $98.92 billion compared to $1.77 billion in net income on $91.23 billion in revenue a year earlier. Stripping out certain one-time items, earnings came in at $1.81 a share. 



  Analysts polled by FactSet expected $94.53 billion in revenue and $1.46 a share in adjusted earnings. 



  CVS raised its adjusted earnings per-share forecast for 2025 to a range between $6.30 to $6.40, up from a prior estimate between $6 a share and $6.20 a share. 



  The Woonsocket, R.I., company's topline results were boosted by double-digit percentage increases across its health care benefits, health services, and pharmacy and consumer wellness segments. The results stand in contrast to financial pressure seen at other health insurers like Centene and UnitedHealth Group as they struggle this year amid a surprise financial squeeze from higher-than-expected medical costs. 



  While CVS's Aetna business is improving, Chief Executive David Joyner notes it continues to face pressure in its health-care delivery business stemming from higher medical benefit ratios at Oak Street Health, which it acquired in 2023. 



  "Earnings were solid across all three segments, but solid growth both in the retail and [health care benefits] segments were key highlights," Mizuho analysts said in a research note. "Although expectations were for a beat, this quarterly beat was higher than expected." 





  Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  July 31, 2025 11:45 ET (15:45 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Optimus Technology Group Names Madhu Maganti as New COO
data.description:
Austin, Texas, July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Optimus Technology Group (https://optimustg.com/), a leading provider of data management, physical security, and power supply services, today announced the appointment of industry veteran Madhu Maganti as Chief Operating Officer, effective July 1. In this role, Maganti will help shape and execute the company’s operational strategy as it accelerates growth across the U.S. and Latin America. He has also been tasked with leading the development of Optimus’s artificial intelligence division, driving innovation to help clients unlock new opportunities for growth and digital transformation.

Maganti joins Optimus Technology Group from Baker Tilly US, where he served as Partner and National Technology Leader, Risk Advisory. Prior to that, he held leadership positions in numerous global management consulting firms and a leading Fortune 50 Technology company.

“Madhu Maganti is an accomplished professional who brings impressive credentials and a forward-thinking mindset to Optimus,” said Sam Tenorio III, President of Optimus Technology Group. “His deep consulting experience, strategic leadership, and operational expertise will be instrumental as we enter our next phase of expansion. I’m confident he will add tremendous value for our clients while scaling our AI and consulting divisions and reinforcing Optimus’s position as a technology leader.”

Throughout his career, Maganti has built and scaled cybersecurity and IT advisory practices, opened new markets, and driven double-digit growth. He is widely recognized as a thought leader, frequently speaking at industry events and contributing to top publications on cybersecurity, risk, and AI governance.

As COO, he will oversee operations across data center development, AI infrastructure, and enterprise IT services, with a focus on delivering sustainable, AI-ready digital infrastructure that supports clients in high-growth markets.

“I’m excited to join a team deeply committed to innovation and digital advancement,” said Maganti. “This is a pivotal time in IT and AI, and I look forward to driving growth and sustainable transformation for our clients and partners.”

Maganti holds a Master’s and Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, and is both a Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA) and a Certified Public Accountant (CPA). He will be based in Houston, Texas.

More information can be found at www.optimustg.com  

About Optimus Technology Group
Formerly known as Seamless Advanced Solutions, Optimus Technology Group is a national IT services firm that offers competitive IT solutions including Professional Services, Managed Services, Communications, Data Centers, Data Management, Physical Security, Power and Artificial Intelligence. Our team is dedicated to shaping the future of data management, security and power supply through innovative technology, turn-key solutions and strategic financial partnerships that empower us to drive the digital evolution across industries. Based in Austin, Texas, the team at Optimus Technology Group has more than 80 years of combined IT expertise.

Attachment

  • Madhu Maganti 

Aaron Cook
Optimus Technology Group
214-520-3430
ACook@tprm.com

data.title: Inbenta Reports Strong 1H 2025 Growth: New Logo Wins, Increased Customer Usage, and Improved Profitability
data.description:
DALLAS, July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Inbenta, an innovator in AI-powered customer experience applications and enterprise automation, today announced significant growth across key business metrics for the first half of 2025. The company reported a 50% year-over-year increase in new customer acquisitions (new logo growth), including continued expansion across European markets. In addition, Inbenta continues to experience growth in its existing customer base: In the first half of 2025, revenue from new services and interactions within existing customer accounts grew 21% year-over-year, driven by greater use of its platform, professional services engagements, and increased adoption of its value-added services.

This increase in usage highlights the growing reliance of Inbenta’s enterprise clients on its AI orchestration platform to power both customer and employee experiences. Enterprise integrations and customer-specific deployments also rose by 54% year-over-year, further demonstrating the platform’s scalability and adaptability across complex environments.

Along with this rapid growth, Inbenta has significantly improved its profitability — meeting internal targets by using its AI to streamline productivity across the organization, while continuing to invest in innovation and customer success, including a major new platform release planned for later this year.

“We’re proud to see strong growth across new logos and deeper usage from our existing customer base,” said Melissa Solis, CEO of Inbenta. “Even more importantly, we achieved this while strengthening our financial foundation. It’s a testament to our team’s focus, our technology’s maturity, and the value we’re delivering to some of the world’s leading enterprises.”

Inbenta’s AI platform helps companies become AI-ready and launch value-driven solutions, providing them with the tools they need to measure and optimize performance. By enabling continuous improvement and scalable impact, Inbenta helps organizations harness AI to create meaningful, efficient connections that proactively boost customer satisfaction, enhance employee productivity, and drive real business growth.

Inbenta is well-positioned for continued momentum as it looks to accelerate its growth trajectory in the second half of 2025 and beyond.

About Inbenta
Inbenta is a global innovator in AI-powered customer and employee experience solutions. Its enterprise AI platform provides personalized, omnichannel support 24/7 through AI Agents that automate interactions and allow human workforces to focus on high-value initiatives. Inbenta enhances engagement, reduces costs, and boosts satisfaction. With deployments in over 100 languages across global enterprises in financial services, e-commerce, government, insurance, technology, healthcare, and travel, Inbenta processes over 2 billion interactions each year.

Media Contact:
marketing@inbenta.com
www.inbenta.com

data.title: Figma's IPO is a 'bellwether event.' Here's why investors want in.
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi and Mike Murphy

Design-software company's market cap tips the scales at more than $19 billion as the IPO priced at $33 a share to raise more than $1.2 billion for the company and shareholders

Design-software company Figma Inc. priced its initial public offering at $33 a share, above what Wall Street was expecting, in a sign of strong investor demand for what has been described by an analyst as a "bellwether event for the tech sector."

That analyst, Derek Hernandez of PitchBook Data, also said Figma (FIG) stands out from other companies that have gone public recently as a "generational software-as-a-service company that has achieved a near-monopolistic hold on the product design market."

Figma has "systematically displaced" larger rivals such as Adobe Inc.'s (ADBE) Adobe XD product and the privately held Sketch BV, Hernandez said.

Adobe tried to buy its fledgling rival for about $20 billion several years ago, but the deal was called off due to concerns about regulatory scrutiny.

The pricing of Figma's IPO at $33 a share topped the projected range of $30 to $32 a share, which had been raised earlier this week from an original range of $25 to $28.

At the IPO price, Figma would have a market capitalization of $19.3 billion, based on 585 million shares outstanding on a fully diluted basis, which includes unvested stock options.

A total of 36.94 million shares were sold in the IPO, raising $1.22 billion. That included the company selling 12.47 million shares to raise $411.7 million, and shareholders selling 24.46 million shares to raise $807.3 million.

Trading of the stock on the New York Stock Exchange is expected to begin later Thursday.

Figma offers investors a "strong and consistent growth rate for a long time" due partly to its international presence and potential domestic expansion, Hernandez said.

He said he wasn't surprised that the underwriters of the deal raised Figma's estimated price range, given the company's high profile as a private software company that had been eyeing an IPO for years before market conditions improved enough in 2025 to move ahead.

"I don't think we've seen a company as good as this for a while," Hernandez told MarketWatch. "It's a very unique opportunity. It would have gone public a while ago in another market."

Read more: Figma files for an IPO. What investors should know about the Adobe design rival.

Experts say Figma's IPO - assuming a positive debut - will likely persuade other private software companies to go public and help re-energize the market. That group could include AI customer-service provider Genesys Cloud Services Inc., graphic-design-software maker Canva Pty Ltd. and data-analytics company Databricks Inc.

"Investors have been starved of new entrants into public markets in recent years," DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria said in a Monday note. "With elongated timelines between capital raises and a liquidity event, these organizations have gotten fit."

Hernandez said the $20 billion valuation by Adobe was like a "line in the sand" for Figma's IPO pricing, so he wasn't surprised when the price target for the IPO was raised on Monday.

With more than 1,600 employees and 13 million monthly active users, Figma products were being used by 95% of the Fortune 500 as of March, with more than half of its revenue coming from outside the U.S.

Dylan Field, co-founder and chief executive of the company, won a Thiel Fellowship to pursue entrepreneurial projects early in his career.

Field is selling 2.35 million shares in the IPO, for proceeds of $77.6 million.

Mamoon Hamid, a board member at Figma and a managing member at venture-capital firm Kleiner Perkins, is selling up to 2.76 million shares, valued at about $91 million.

Other principal shareholders of Figma include affiliates of Index Ventures, Greylock Partners and Sequoia Capital.

The IPO from Figma comes after several successful larger IPOs this year, including AI data-center specialist CoreWeave Inc. CRWV, payments-technology company Circle Internet Group Inc. (CRCL), online brokerage eToro Group Ltd. (ETOR) and online banking-services company Chime Financial Inc. (CHYM).

More: Ambiq's stock soars after IPO. Here's where the company sees its place in the AI boom.

-Steve Gelsi -Mike Murphy

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  07-31-25 1138ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Call for Speakers: The NAMA Show 2026
data.description:
Call for Speakers: The NAMA Show 2026

Proposals Open Through September 16

The National Automatic Merchandising Association (NAMA) is now accepting speaker proposals for The NAMA Show 2026, taking place April 22-24 in Los Angeles.

This high-impact educational leadership opportunity targets over 5,200 professionals in convenience services who join The NAMA Show. This is the opportunity for experts in client relations, operational optimization, service innovation, and retail design to demonstrate their subject matter expertise to an audience of decision-makers.

Why present at The NAMA Show?

  • Visibility. Gain traction as a recognized expert in front of operations directors, sales and marketing professionals, technology experts, and business owners across unattended retail and self-serve convenience services: vending, micro markets, coffee and pantry services, and more.
  • Connection. Build relationships with decision-makers who are actively investing in solutions and strategies for growth.
  • Impact. Contribute to the industry’s future by sharing real-world experience, emerging insights, and innovative approaches.

Who should apply?

The NAMA Show welcomes speakers who are solving real challenges, transforming operations, or pushing the boundaries of unattended retail and self-service. NAMA is seeking:

  • Operators achieving breakthrough results in vending, micro markets, pantry, or office coffee service (OCS).
  • Suppliers and tech innovators reshaping the self-service experience.
  • Recognized experts in retail and convenience who can articulate the roadmap to success.
  • Experts from healthcare, hospitality, higher education, or retail integrating unattended solutions.
  • Brand leaders and marketers creating powerful, consumer-first experiences.
  • Strong communicators who can deliver clear, valuable guidance on the fundamentals of a good operation.

Proposals should focus on helping operators drive business results, whether through profitability, efficiency, or better customer experience. Topics should be relevant and designed for audiences such as business leaders, operations directors, sales professionals, and more.

Speaker proposals are due September 16. Learn more and submit through The NAMA Show website. Selected speakers will be notified by the end of November 2025.

###

NAMA represents the U.S. Convenience Services Industry, which contributes $40.04 billion to the U.S. economy. By providing advocacy, education, and research, NAMA works to promote and protect the industry’s over 165,000 hardworking employees. Through traditional vending and micro markets, office coffee and pantry services, product manufacturing, and small-drop distribution, convenience services meets the needs of over 40 million American consumers daily at work, home, school, and play.

Sandra Renderos, srenderos@namanow.org

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250731602832/en/

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 11 AM ET: Oil Futures Pull Back After Three-Day Rally | U.S. ...
data.description:
Oil Futures Pull Back After Three-Day Rally

Oil futures fell after a three-day rally driven by a spate of U.S. trade deals ahead of Friday's tariff deadline and threats of sanctions against Russia and buyers of its oil.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Struggles to Hold Above $3

Natural gas futures fell, hovering around $3 with cooler weather through early next week and continued weakness in LNG feedgas flows weighing.

----

Major Banks Nudge Oil Forecast Higher But U.S. Tariffs, OPEC+ Hikes Weigh on Outlook

Major Wall Street banks have modestly lifted their oil-price forecasts for this year and next, but lingering uncertainty over U.S. tariffs and rising OPEC+ output continue to cloud the outlook.

----

Schneider Electric's Double-Digit Growth Driven by Data Center Deals

The company said it remains well placed to seize opportunities in its end markets, despite an environment of heightened uncertainty.

----

Shell Keeps $3.5 Billion Buyback as Earnings Fall Less Than Expected

The oil major maintains the pace of shareholder returns despite lower prices weighing on energy companies' balance sheets.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Shell, crude futures, Seatrium and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Post Large Weekly Build

U.S. crude oil inventories rose sharply last week as domestic production and imports increased and exports fell, according to data released by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

----

Baker Hughes to Acquire Chart Industries for $13.6 Billion

Baker Hughes said the buy will advance its strategic vision, expand its offerings in attractive growth markets and complement its existing capability.

----

Who Pays? AI Boom Sparks Fight Over Soaring Power Costs

Utilities and technology companies are at odds over who should pay for electricity costs in unprecedented data-center build-out.

----

America Needs More Power. Its Warehouse Rooftops Could Provide the Solution.

SolarEdge and Solar Landscape plan to fit more than 500 warehouses with a combined photovoltaic installation of 630 megawatts, enough to power 80,000 homes.

----

Trump's $750 Billion Deal for U.S. Energy Collides With Market Reality

Europe has pledged to increase purchases of American oil, natural gas and nuclear fuels , but exporters might struggle to meet demand.

----

Venture Global Closes $15 Billion Financing for Gas Exports to Europe

Financing is secured for the first phase of a project that will feed the flow of U.S. natural-gas exports to Europe.

----

EU Launches Investigation Into Adnoc's $13.7 Billion Acquisition of Covestro

The European Commission cited concerns that foreign subsidies from the United Arab Emirates could distort the European market.

Moderna Cuts 10% of Its Staff as Cash Hoard Dwindles

Kellanova's Profit Falls as Challenges Continue
